{{Infobox character
| name = Sadesthenes
| appears_in = The Elder Empire: Sea
| titles = philosopher, historian, classic author
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[Sadesthenes Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Sadesthenes is a renowned philosopher, historian, and ancient figure whose foundational works remain central to classical studies. Known by various titles including the Father of Knowledge, the [[Lord]] of a [[Thousand Eyes]], and He who knows all, he is described as an [[Elder]] whose pursuit of total knowledge was his primary motivation. While his desire to learn everything meant the survival of humanity was not his priority, he lacked the malicious intent toward mankind seen in other entities. His [[life]] is tied to [[Imperial]] history, and he was once the [[Steward]] of the [[Aurelian Empire]]. Despite his status as a great thinker, some accounts claim he was actually the self-taught son of a wine merchant. His teachings cover a wide range of topics, including leadership, war, emotion, and the inevitability of certain outcomes, and his wisdom is sometimes considered forbidden due to its association with death.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Sadesthenes is a philosopher known for his discourse on wisdom, gold, and the transient nature of all things. His teachings include the belief that a successful person visualizes success instead of failure and the principle that one should prioritize the preservation of [[life]] when in doubt. Regarding conflict, he argued that while fools seek combat, wise men remain at home, a philosophy later quoted by [[Calder]]. His political and strategic insights further suggest that the path toward unity leads inevitably to a single [[Empire]] and that a [[Reader]]'s weapons are found all around them.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] is a person who once spoke about wisdom and gold.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c1">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] is known for the saying: 'The fool seeks out combat, but the wise man stays at home.'<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] is a figure whose words regarding governance and war remain true.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] provides philosophical advice.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] is a philosopher.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c6">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] once said that the path to unity can only end in one [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] is a figure quoted by [[Calder]] regarding combat philosophy.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c17">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] said that a successful man visualizes success rather than failure.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c20">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] once said that a [[Reader]]'s weapons are all around them.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] said, 'When in doubt, seek first to preserve [[life]].'<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Sadesthenes]] once said that nothing lasts forever.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Sadesthenes]] was a great historian and philosopher.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c5">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Sadesthenes]] is a source of wisdom.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A contemporary of [[Sadesthenes]] wrote that a man shows weakness when he casts shame on his ancestors.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c15">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Sadesthenes]] is a classic author.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 16</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Sadesthenes once observed that the words of a genuine leader are difficult to overlook. He maintained that wisdom involves acting without hesitation when necessity arises and believed that being an [[Emperor]] required carrying the weight of the entire [[Empire]]. While he argued that a wise person prepares for death without actively planning to die, he also authored the sentiment that mercy is an exceptionally rare virtue among kings and killers.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sadesthenes]] once wrote that a true leader's words are not easily ignored.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c2">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sadesthenes]] said that a wise man does not hesitate when faced with necessity.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c9">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sadesthenes]] said, 'To be [[the Emperor]] is to carry the [[Empire]].'<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sadesthenes]] said, "The wise man plans for his death, but does not plan to die."<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Sadesthenes]] is the author of the quote 'The quality of mercy is among the rarest of virtues, and rarest of all in killers and kings.'<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Sadesthenes once said, “The wise man is not blinded by gold, but only a fool turns it down."<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c1" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“The fool seeks out combat, but the wise man stays at home.” One of the most well-known sayings of Sadesthenes.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|"Anger is a cruel thief," Sadesthenes said.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Sadesthenes once said, "As it is in the hearts of men to seek authority, the path to unity can only end in one Empire."<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|"It was Sadesthenes who first said, ‘In most of life, to fight is to lose.’"}, {<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c17" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire characters]]
[[Category:Readers]]
